summary: Re-enabled the C implementation of sysdolphin/baselib/pobj.c for linking by changing pobj from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py, switching obj_files.mk from the asm pobj.s object to the src pobj.c object, and deleting the obsolete asm/sysdolphin/baselib/pobj.s fallback. The PR body had no explanatory text, but the diff and review make clear this was a build/linkage cleanup after pobj became matchable.
what_changed: Deleted asm/sysdolphin/baselib/pobj.s, removing 4236 lines of handwritten/disassembled assembly fallback for pobj.c.
Changed configure.py RuntimeLib entry for sysdolphin/baselib/pobj.c from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...).
Changed obj_files.mk TEXT_O_FILES entry from $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/sysdolphin/baselib/pobj.s.o to $(BUILD_DIR)/src/sysdolphin/baselib/pobj.c.o.

The deleted assembly contained exported and local pobj-related symbols such as HSD_PObjGetFlags, HSD_PObjRemoveAnimAllByFlags, HSD_PObjAddAnimAll, HSD_PObjReqAnimAllByFlags, PObjUpdateFunc, HSD_PObjAnimAll, loadEnvelopeDesc, loadShapeSetDesc, PObjLoad, HSD_PObjLoadDesc, HSD_PObjRemoveAll, HSD_PObjSetDefaultClass, and HSD_PObjAlloc; this PR itself did not modify their C source in the provided diff.
smart_moves: Kept the re-enable minimal: only the matching status, object-file path, and obsolete asm fallback changed.
Updated both build description layers that mattered in this repository slice: configure.py and obj_files.mk.
Removed the old asm file instead of leaving stale fallback code after switching obj_files.mk to src/sysdolphin/baselib/pobj.c.o.
Responded to review feedback promptly: a reviewer requested deleting pobj.s, and the final state includes pobj.s deleted.
decomp_lessons: When promoting a file from NonMatching to Matching, update configure.py so the decomp build treats the C file as linkable.
Also update obj_files.mk to replace the asm object with the src C object; changing only configure.py is not enough in this build setup.
Delete the corresponding asm fallback once the C file is linked, both to avoid stale code and to satisfy project hygiene expectations.
A PR that only re-enables linking may have a very small diff outside the removed asm; the important evidence is the object selection change rather than C source edits.
Reviewer feedback can focus on repository cleanup rather than matching logic; here the explicit requested cleanup was deletion of pobj.s.
naming_conventions: PObj/baselib symbols use the HSD_PObj* prefix for public functions, matching surrounding HSD baselib naming.
Local helper names in the deleted asm used PObjUpdateFunc, PObjLoad, loadEnvelopeDesc, and loadShapeSetDesc; these names are evidence from the removed assembly, not newly introduced names in this PR.
Build paths consistently use sysdolphin/baselib/pobj.c for the source implementation and asm/sysdolphin/baselib/pobj.s for the fallback assembly.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Promotion tactic: switch Object(NonMatching, "sysdolphin/baselib/pobj.c") to Object(Matching, "sysdolphin/baselib/pobj.c") in configure.py.
Linkage tactic: replace $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/sysdolphin/baselib/pobj.s.o with $(BUILD_DIR)/src/sysdolphin/baselib/pobj.c.o in obj_files.mk.
Cleanup tactic: remove the entire asm/sysdolphin/baselib/pobj.s file after enabling the matching C object.
No specific C-level matching tricks are visible in this PR slice because the C source diff is not included and appears unchanged.
follow_up_queries: Look for the earlier PR or commit that made sysdolphin/baselib/pobj.c match before this re-enable.
Search for other baselib files still marked NonMatching in configure.py but close to being linkable.
Audit whether obj_files.mk and configure.py are synchronized for other promoted Matching objects.
Check if any references or documentation still mention asm/sysdolphin/baselib/pobj.s after its deletion.
